name: Particle Filter Skeleton
summary: Resample particles when needed, propagate each particle through the transition model, score the observation likelihood, then normalize and summarize the posterior particle state.
dejargonized_summary: Keep a cloud of candidate states, move them forward, compare each one against the new measurement, and keep the weighted cloud instead of collapsing uncertainty too early.

stages.name: Preprocess
stages.description: Compute effective sample size and resample the particles when the current weight distribution has become too degenerate.
stages.dejargonized_description: Check whether the particle cloud has collapsed too much and refresh it before the next step if needed.

stages.name: Predict
stages.description: Propagate each particle independently through the transition model before incorporating the new observation.
stages.dejargonized_description: Move every candidate state forward one step before scoring it against the measurement.

stages.name: Reweight
stages.description: Score each predicted particle against the observation and convert those scores into updated particle weights.
stages.dejargonized_description: Ask how well each candidate state explains the new observation, then turn those answers into weights.

stages.name: Postprocess
stages.description: Normalize the weights, package the posterior particle set, and compute the state estimate exposed downstream.
stages.dejargonized_description: Turn the weighted particle cloud back into the updated estimate and keep the particle state available for the next step.

planning_constraints.subject: particle_filter
planning_constraints.statement: Preserve the weighted particle state until the posterior packaging stage finishes.
planning_constraints.rationale: Particle-filter families rely on particle diversity and weight evolution remaining explicit across the update boundary.

audit.rationale: Publishes a concrete particle-filter macro scaffold while artifact ownership migrates into the provider repos.
audit.dejargonized_summary: A concrete sequential Monte Carlo scaffold that keeps resampling, propagation, likelihood scoring, and posterior packaging separate.

migration_readiness.rationale: The particle-filter topology is stable, but the canonical artifact still lives in the matcher compatibility layer.

references.title: Sequential Monte Carlo Methods in Practice
references.citation: Doucet, de Freitas, Gordon. Particle filtering reference collection.

uncertainty_notes: This scaffold does not encode proposal-specific auxiliary branches or Rao-Blackwellized substructure.
